dc_independence.note: DC cross-talk is compliance-independent by construction; near-zero change confirms the coupling drift is purely dynamic.

most_sensitive_frequency.selection_note: ranked by absolute change in |H21| (measurable neighbor-pressure response), not the saturating |H21/H11| ratio.

interpretation: Spine holds in simulation: inter-chamber cross-talk drifts monotonically with chamber-1 compliance. The signal is dynamic (DC gain is compliance-independent), strongest near 1.6 Hz, usable across 0.29-5.7 Hz. EXPERIMENTAL IMPLICATION: actuate/probe in this band and use history-dependent (ARX/ESN) features; a static ridge map on instantaneous pressure will not see the fatigue->cross-talk coupling. Green-light the Week-3 hardware gate.
